message:
chore: silence compiler warnings for unused code

This commit addresses remaining compiler warnings without changing
any program behavior. All changes are cosmetic annotations that
signal intentional decisions to the compiler.

UNUSED VARIABLE WARNINGS (src/main.rs):
Prefixed intentionally unused variables with underscore per Rust
convention. These variables are opened/initialized but not yet used
because the features are partially implemented:

- cmd_search(): `storage` → `_storage`
  The Storage handle is opened to verify the database exists and is
  valid, but search currently uses only the Tantivy index. Reserved
  for future hybrid search features (SQL + full-text).

- cmd_stats(): `args` → `_args`
  The StatsArgs struct contains fields like `detailed`, `hashtags`,
  `mentions`, and `top` that are parsed but not yet implemented.
  The basic stats display works; detailed breakdowns are planned.

- cmd_export(): `cli` → `_cli`, `args` → `_args`
  Export command is stubbed out with a "not implemented" message.
  Both parameters will be used when export functionality is added.

DEAD CODE WARNING (src/search.rs):
Added #[allow(dead_code)] to DocType::from_str() method. This is a
symmetric companion to as_str() that provides the inverse mapping.
While not currently called, it's part of the type's public API
contract and will be useful for deserializing document types from
stored strings. Keeping it maintains API symmetry and prevents
reimplementation when needed.

TEST COMPILATION FIX (src/parser.rs):
Added `use chrono::Datelike;` import to the test module. The test
`test_parse_x_date()` calls `.year()`, `.month()`, and `.day()` on
DateTime values, which require the Datelike trait to be in scope.
This was exposed when unused imports were cleaned up in the previous
commit, as TimeZone was previously re-exporting Datelike indirectly.

VERIFICATION:
After these changes, `cargo build` and `cargo test` both complete
with zero warnings:

    Finished `dev` profile [optimized + debuginfo] target(s)
    running 5 tests ... test result: ok. 5 passed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
